details: :edit {file} opens a file for editing
Write first or use :edit! if you want to discard current changes
:find searches Vim's 'path' and edits the match
Useful when the file is not in the current directory
:browse opens the file-open dialog
Use it when you want to pick a file instead of typing its name
:buffers, :files, and :ls list known files/buffers
Use the list before switching with :buffer, :bn, :bp, or Ctrl-^

tips.summary: Jump to a listed buffer with :buffer {N}
details: :buffer {N} jumps to the numbered buffer from the buffer list
Use it after :ls when you know exactly which file you want

tips.summary: Step through the argument list with :next / :previous
details: :next moves to the next file in the argument list
:previous moves back to the earlier one

tips.summary: Jump to a specific argument with :argument {N}
details: :argument {N} edits the Nth file in the argument list
Use it when you know exactly which argument-list file you want

tips.summary: Jump to the ends of the argument list with :first / :last
details: :first edits the first file in the argument list
:last edits the last file in the argument list
Jumps to the alternate file
Great for back-and-forth edits
Ctrl-^ is Ctrl-6 on many keyboards
:bn goes to the next buffer
:bp goes to the previous buffer
:bd closes the current buffer/editor tab
Use it when you are done with the file
:e reloads if no changes
:e! discards changes and reloads
:w writes the file
In IdeaVim, this maps to IDE save

tips.summary: Write a copy to another file :w {file}
details: :w {file} writes the current buffer to a different file
Useful when you want a copy without changing the current buffer name
:wa writes all modified buffers
In IDE, typically saves all open editors
:wnext writes the current file and moves to the next one
:wprevious writes and moves to the previous file

tips.summary: Read a file into the current buffer with :read {file}
details: :read {file} inserts another file below the current line
Useful when you want to pull file contents into the current buffer
:q closes if no unsaved changes
:q! forces close/discard (if allowed)
:wq writes and quits
:x writes only if changed
ZZ also writes and quits the current file
:qa exits unless there are changed buffers
:qall closes all editors
Use the force form only if you really want to discard pending changes
:wqa writes changed buffers and exits
:xa is the equivalent all-files write-and-exit command

tips.summary: Home-row movement h j k l
details: h left, j down, k up, l right
Use counts like 10j to jump

tips.summary: Use counts before motions and operators
details: Put a number before a motion or operator
5j moves down 5 lines
3dw deletes 3 words
G goes to the last line
{count}G goes to that line number
Example: 120G jumps to line 120
w next word start
b previous word start
e moves to end of word
ge goes to end of previous word
Nice for edits before punctuation

tips.summary: Big WORD moves W / B / E
details: WORDs split on whitespace
Great for paths and long tokens
gE goes to end of previous WORD
Useful on URLs / file paths
0 start of line
^ first non-blank
$ end of line
g_ jumps to last non-space character
Handy near );}] and trailing spaces

tips.summary: Move to first text on next/prev line + / -
details: + moves down to first non-blank
- moves up to first non-blank
These are motions; use with operators (e.g., d+)
80| jumps to column 80
Great for line-length edits
gg goes to first line
G goes to last line
Use a count before % to jump by file percentage
50% jumps to middle of file
10% jumps near the top
Bare % jumps to a matching pair instead

tips.summary: Jump to top/middle/bottom of screen H / M / L
details: H top of screen, M middle, L bottom
Great for fast scanning

tips.summary: Move by screen lines on wraps gj / gk
details: gj and gk follow wrapped screen lines
Use them when one long line wraps

tips.summary: Screen-line start/end on wraps g0 / g^ / g$
details: g0 goes to screen-line start
g^ to first text, g$ to screen-line end
{ goes to previous paragraph
} goes to next paragraph
Moves across blank-line-separated blocks
( moves to the previous sentence start
) moves to the next sentence start
Useful in prose, comments, and doc blocks
% jumps between (), {}, and [] by default
Use a count like 50% to jump by file percentage instead
Use to inspect a whole block quickly
`` returns to the exact previous spot
'' returns to the previous line
Useful after big jumps like G, %, /, or file switches
Ctrl-o goes back through older jump locations
Ctrl-i goes forward again
Useful after searches, definitions, and other big jumps
`. jumps to the exact last change
'. jumps to the line of the last change
Useful when you want to get back to where you were editing

tips.summary: Find a character forward on the line f{c}
details: f; jumps to next ';' on the line
Great for long parameter lists

tips.summary: Find a character backward on the line F{c}
details: F; jumps to previous ';' on the line
Use to move precisely without searching

tips.summary: Move till before a character t{c}
details: t) stops just before ')'
Perfect for ct) edits

tips.summary: Move backward till after a character T{c}
details: T) stops just after ')'
Good for edits near opening brackets
; repeats in same direction
, repeats in opposite direction

tips.summary: Place line top/center/bottom zt zz zb
details: zt top, zz center, zb bottom
Use after searches and big jumps

tips.summary: Place first text z<CR> z. z-
details: z<CR> top, z. center, z- bottom
Moves to first non-blank on that line

tips.summary: Page then place first text z+ / z^
details: z+ uses next page, z^ previous page
Then lands at first non-blank
Ctrl-d scrolls down half a page
Ctrl-u scrolls up half a page
Ctrl-f scrolls forward one page
Ctrl-b scrolls back one page
Ctrl-e scrolls down one line
Ctrl-y scrolls up one line

tips.summary: Scroll sideways by columns zh / zl
details: zh scrolls left, zl scrolls right
Needs wrap off or soft-wrap off

tips.summary: Scroll sideways half screen zH / zL
details: zH and zL scroll half a screen
Needs wrap off or soft-wrap off

tips.summary: Place cursor screen edge zs / ze
details: zs shows cursor at screen start
ze shows cursor at screen end
Needs wrap off or soft-wrap off
:set so=5 keeps context lines
Use sidescrolloff for side context

tips.summary: Set Ctrl-d / Ctrl-u size with scroll
details: :set scroll=15 changes step size
Useful when half-page feels too big
